crime_types.crime_name: Online Financial Fraud / Cyber Fraud / Payment Scam
crime_types.description: Unauthorized digital transaction resulting in money loss through phishing, UPI fraud, fake websites, lottery scams, or online payment scams

it_act_sections.title: Punishment for Identity Theft
it_act_sections.penalty: Imprisonment up to 3 years + fine up to ₹1 lakh — for fraudulently using victim's credentials, OTP, or banking identity to access their account

it_act_sections.title: Punishment for Cheating by Personation using Computer Resource
it_act_sections.penalty: Imprisonment up to 3 years + fine up to ₹1 lakh — for impersonating bank officials, RBI officers, or support staff using phone/digital means to extract money

it_act_sections.title: Computer Related Offences
it_act_sections.penalty: Imprisonment up to 3 years + fine up to ₹5 lakh — for fraudulently accessing victim's bank/payment accounts via digital means

it_act_sections.title: Penalty for Unauthorized Access to Computer System
it_act_sections.penalty: Civil compensation to victim — for unauthorized access to banking computer systems or payment infrastructure

it_act_sections.title: Punishment for Disclosure of Information in Breach of Lawful Contract
it_act_sections.penalty: Imprisonment up to 3 years + fine up to ₹5 lakh — applicable if bank/payment insider leaked victim's financial data to fraudster

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
ipc_sections.penalty: Up to 7 years + fine — generic online payment fraud

ipc_sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust
ipc_sections.penalty: Up to 3 years + fine — misappropriation of entrusted money

ipc_sections.title: Cheating by Impersonation
ipc_sections.penalty: Up to 3 years + fine — impersonating bank/official

immediate_actions: TURANT karo: Apne bank ke customer care ko ABHI call karo — transaction freeze ya reversal request karo (number card ke peeche hota hai)
TURANT karo: Net Banking / UPI app mein jaao — apna UPI PIN aur net banking password ABHI change karo
Cyber Helpline 1930 pe call karo — India ka official financial cyber fraud helpline, 24x7 available
National Cyber Crime Portal pe complaint file karo: cybercrime.gov.in
Apne nearest police station mein FIR file karo with all evidence
evidence_to_collect: Bank statement showing fraudulent transaction (PDF download karo)
SMS/Email screenshots of OTP, alerts, or suspicious messages
Call logs showing fraudster's phone number
Screenshots of fake apps/websites/links
UPI transaction IDs and payment confirmation screens
WhatsApp/Telegram chat screenshots with fraudster
crime_types.legal_summary: This is a case of Online Financial Fraud punishable under IT Act 2000 (Sections 43, 66, 66C, 66D, 72A) and Indian Penal Code (Sections 419, 420, 406). Maximum combined punishment can extend to 7 years imprisonment with heavy fines.

cross_questions.question: How was the money taken from your account?

skip_logic.reason: User already mentioned HOW money was taken (method/platform)
skip_logic.auto_extract_answer: Extract method from user's initial message and use as answer

options: UPI transfer (PhonePe, GPay, Paytm, Google Pay, BHIM)
Net banking / IMPS / NEFT / RTGS transfer
QR code scan / Payment request / Payment link
Someone asked me to transfer money (fake customer care/police)
Clicked a link and entered card/bank details

cross_questions.question: When did you first notice the money was gone?

skip_logic.auto_extract_answer: Extract time reference and categorize into one of the options

options: Just now / Within last 2 hours (EMERGENCY - Call bank NOW! 🚨)
Today / Within last 24 hours (Call bank IMMEDIATELY)
More than a week ago (but less than 3 months)

cross_questions.question: Have you contacted your bank about this?

skip_logic.reason: User mentioned if they contacted bank or not
skip_logic.auto_extract_answer: Check if user said yes/no to bank contact

options: Yes - but they said nothing can be done / too late
No - haven't contacted yet (DO IT NOW! Time is critical! ⏰)
No - don't know bank number / how to contact

evidence_to_collect: Screenshots of fraudulent SMS/messages/emails
Transaction details (date, time, amount, UPI ID/account number)
Bank statement showing unauthorized debit
Screenshots of fake website/app
Call recording (if scammer called)
Caller ID / Phone number of scammer
Email headers (if phishing email)
Chat screenshots (WhatsApp/Telegram/Instagram)
Any documents shared by scammer
Your device details (for forensics if needed)
immediate_actions: 🚨 CALL YOUR BANK IMMEDIATELY (Customer care number on back of card / 1800 number)
Ask bank to FREEZE receiving account (give them UPI ID / account number where money went)
File complaint on National Cyber Crime Portal: cybercrime.gov.in
Call National Cyber Helpline: 1930 (24x7 toll-free)
DO NOT delete any messages/emails - they are evidence
Block your card if card details were shared
Change all passwords if credentials were compromised
Check transaction history for any other unauthorized transactions

case_study.title: Mumbai Cyber Cell 2023 - UPI Phishing Recovery Case
case_study.facts: 45-year-old victim received SMS: 'Dear SBI customer, your account will be blocked. Update KYC: [link]'. Clicked link, entered 16-digit card number, CVV, and OTP. Within 2 minutes, Rs. 45,000 transferred via UPI to unknown PhonePe number.
case_study.timeline: Victim noticed SMS alert 5 minutes later. Called SBI customer care within 20 minutes. Bank froze receiving account immediately.
case_study.police_action: Cyber cell tracked UPI transaction ID to PhonePe account → Bank account → CCTV footage of ATM where accused tried to withdraw. Arrested within 48 hours from Jharkhand.
case_study.conviction: IT Act 66C (Identity Theft) + 66D (Cheating by impersonation) + IPC 420 (Cheating). Sentenced to 3 years RI + Rs. 50,000 fine.
case_study.recovery: Rs. 27,000 recovered (60% of amount). Remaining Rs. 18,000 already withdrawn via ATM within first hour.
case_study.key_lesson: ⏰ TIME IS EVERYTHING! Report within 2 hours = 70-80% recovery chance. After 24 hours = only 10-20% recovery. Fraudsters withdraw money FAST via ATM/UPI.

crime_types.crime_name: Social Media / Email Account Hacking / Unauthorized Account Access
crime_types.description: Unauthorized access to email, Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp, Snapchat, Twitter, or other online accounts without owner's permission

it_act_sections.title: Computer related offences / Hacking with computer system
it_act_sections.penalty: Up to 3 years imprisonment + Fine up to Rs. 5 lakh

it_act_sections.title: Identity theft (if account used for fraud)
it_act_sections.penalty: Up to 3 years imprisonment + Fine up to Rs. 1 lakh

skip_logic.auto_extract_answer: Extract platform name from initial message

cross_questions.question: Can you access your account at all right now?

skip_logic.auto_extract_answer: Check if user can/cannot access account

options: Completely locked out - can't login at all ❌
Can login but hacker changed password/settings
Can view but can't post/delete/change things
Account deleted or deactivated by hacker
Account is accessible but suspicious activity visible
Not sure - haven't tried yet / afraid to login

cross_questions.question: Do you still have access to your recovery email or registered phone number?

skip_logic.auto_extract_answer: Check if recovery options available or changed

options: Yes - I have access to both (email + phone) ✅
Yes - Only recovery email (phone changed by hacker)
Yes - Only phone number (email changed by hacker)
No - Hacker changed both email and phone number ❌
Not sure - need to check recovery options

cross_questions.question: Did the hacker send messages/posts from your account or ask your contacts for money?

skip_logic.reason: User already mentioned misuse of hacked account
skip_logic.auto_extract_answer: Extract if account misuse happened and what type

options: Yes - Hacker sent money requests to my contacts
Yes - Hacker posted/scammed from my account
Not sure - need to verify with contacts

evidence_to_collect: Screenshots of 'unusual activity' or 'new login' notifications
Email from platform about password change / email change
Screenshots of unauthorized posts / messages / stories
Login history / Active sessions (if accessible)
Any suspicious emails / messages received before hack
Screenshots of changed profile (if visible to friends)
Friends' screenshots of suspicious DMs from your account
Recovery codes / backup codes (if you have them)
Last known password (to prove it's your account)
Original account creation details (signup date, old photos)
immediate_actions: 🔐 Try account recovery immediately (Forgot Password / Can't access account)
Check all connected email accounts for password reset emails
Report to platform: Instagram/Facebook/Gmail Help Center
Enable 2FA / Two-factor authentication (if you recover account)
Warn friends/followers about hack (post from another account / call them)
Change passwords of ALL other accounts using same password
File complaint on cybercrime.gov.in
Contact National Cyber Helpline: 1930
Check if hacker posted/messaged anything - screenshot it
DO NOT pay any ransom if hacker demands money

Instagram.method: instagram.com/accounts/password/reset/ OR 'Get help signing in' on app
Instagram.timeline: 24-48 hours for manual review if auto-recovery fails

Facebook.method: facebook.com/hacked OR 'Forgot Password' → 'My account is compromised'
Facebook.requirements: Trusted contacts OR Government ID verification

Gmail.requirements: Recovery email/phone OR last password OR account creation date
Gmail.timeline: Instant if recovery options work, 3-5 days for manual review

WhatsApp.method: Reinstall WhatsApp with your number → Request verification code
WhatsApp.requirements: SMS/Call access to your phone number
WhatsApp.timeline: Instant - WhatsApp can only be active on one device (unless linked devices feature used)

case_study.title: Delhi Cyber Crime 2023 - Instagram Hack with Extortion
case_study.facts: 22-year-old student's Instagram hacked via phishing link that looked like 'Verify your account' message. Hacker changed email, phone, and password. Then sent DM from victim's account demanding Rs. 10,000: 'Pay or I'll post your private chats publicly'.
case_study.victim_action: Victim immediately: (1) Reported to Instagram, (2) Posted warning from friend's account, (3) Filed cyber complaint with screenshots, (4) Did NOT pay ransom.
case_study.police_action: Cyber cell sent legal notice to Instagram parent company Meta. Instagram provided login IP logs. Tracked to internet cafe in Jharkhand. Accused arrested in 5 days.
case_study.evidence_used: Instagram login IP logs, screenshots of extortion messages, email headers of phishing message, cafe CCTV footage.
case_study.conviction: IT Act 66 (Hacking) + 66C (Identity Theft) + IPC 384 (Extortion) + IPC 419 (Cheating by personation). Sentenced to 2 years RI.
case_study.recovery: Account recovered after 7 days. No money lost as victim didn't pay.
case_study.key_lesson: 🚫 NEVER PAY HACKERS. Report immediately to platform + police. Most platforms cooperate with law enforcement. Instagram/Facebook/Gmail do respond to legal requests from Indian cyber cells.

crime_types.crime_name: Sextortion / Online Blackmail / Intimate Image Abuse
crime_types.description: Threatening to share intimate images, videos, or private information unless payment made or demands met. Includes revenge porn and non-consensual sharing of private content.

it_act_sections.title: Publishing obscene material in electronic form
it_act_sections.penalty: First offense: Up to 3 years + Rs. 5 lakh fine. Second offense: Up to 5 years + Rs. 10 lakh fine

it_act_sections.title: Publishing sexually explicit content in electronic form
it_act_sections.penalty: First offense: Up to 5 years + Rs. 10 lakh fine. Second offense: Up to 7 years + Rs. 10 lakh fine

it_act_sections.title: Violation of privacy (capturing/transmitting private images)
it_act_sections.penalty: Up to 3 years imprisonment + Rs. 2 lakh fine

ipc_sections.title: Putting person in fear of injury to commit extortion
Criminal intimidation (if threat to cause death/grievous hurt)
Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman

cross_questions.question: Where did this threat happen? (Which platform/app?)
When did they FIRST threaten you?
options: Today / Within last 24 hours (URGENT - Report NOW!) 🚨
More than 2 weeks ago (but less than 3 months)
More than 3 months ago / Ongoing for long time

cross_questions.question: Have you already done any of these?
options: None of the above - haven't done anything yet

cross_questions.question: How did they get the intimate content?

skip_logic.reason: User explained how content was obtained

options: I shared willingly in a relationship / consensual
During video call - they recorded without permission
They hacked my account / device and stole photos
Don't know how they got it
They don't actually have anything - just bluffing

evidence_to_collect: 🚫 DO NOT DELETE ANY MESSAGES - They are critical evidence!
Screenshots of ALL threatening messages (with timestamps visible)
Screenshots showing their profile / username / phone number
Call recordings if they called (India allows recording your own calls)
Transaction screenshots if you paid (bank transfer / UPI receipt)
Screenshots of ANY content they claim to have
Email headers if threats came via email
Details of how you know this person (dating app match, social media, etc.)
Timeline document (when first contact, when threat started, etc.)
Witness statements if someone else knows about this
immediate_actions: 🚫 DO NOT PAY - Payment never stops blackmail, it escalates it!
🚫 DO NOT DELETE any messages / chats - Critical evidence!
🚫 DO NOT block yet - Police need to track their account!
✅ Screenshot EVERYTHING immediately (messages, profile, threats)
✅ Report on National Cyber Crime Portal: cybercrime.gov.in (can file anonymously)
✅ Call National Cyber Helpline: 1930 (24x7, counselors available)
✅ Consider telling trusted family member / friend for emotional support
✅ If threats are very recent (24 hours), go to nearest cyber cell IMMEDIATELY
✅ File FIR at women's police station (for women victims) or cyber cell
🔒 Make all social media accounts private temporarily
🔒 Change passwords if they have any of your account access
⚠️ If they actually post content - report to platform immediately + file complaint

victim_support_resources.name: National Commission for Women (NCW) Cyber Crime Cell
victim_support_resources.for: Women victims - Free legal aid + counseling

case_study.title: Karnataka High Court 2023 - Dating App Sextortion with Arrest
case_study.facts: 22-year-old woman matched with man on Tinder. After chatting for a week, had consensual video call. During call, accused recorded intimate moments without victim's knowledge. Next day, received WhatsApp message: 'I have your video. Pay Rs. 5 lakh or I'll send to your family and office.' Included screenshots proving he had video.
case_study.victim_response: Victim DID NOT PAY. Instead: (1) Immediately reported on cybercrime.gov.in within 6 hours, (2) Preserved all evidence (screenshots, profile details), (3) Told parents for emotional support, (4) Filed FIR at women's police station.
case_study.police_investigation: Cyber cell sent legal notice to WhatsApp (Meta) + Tinder (Match Group) requesting data. Got IP logs, device info, email, payment details. Tracked accused to different state (Karnataka to Jharkhand). Also found 15 other victims.
case_study.arrest_timeline: Accused arrested within 72 hours using digital trail: Instagram IP → ISP data → Physical location → CCTV → Arrest.
case_study.evidence: WhatsApp chat backups, Tinder match data, victim's statement, IP logs, accused's phone with 15 other victims' videos.
case_study.charges: IT Act 67A (Sexually explicit content) + 66E (Privacy violation) + IPC 384 (Extortion) + IPC 506 (Criminal intimidation) + IPC 509 (Outraging modesty)
case_study.sentence: 5 years rigorous imprisonment + Rs. 2 lakh fine. Court ordered fine amount to be paid to victim as compensation.
case_study.outcomes_for_15_victims: Police contacted all 15 victims. 12 had paid (total Rs. 18 lakh extorted). Videos deleted during investigation. Accused's devices forensically wiped.
case_study.victim_outcome: Victim recovered fully. Video never shared. Case solved in 10 days from complaint to arrest.
critical_lessons: ⏰ Quick action within 24 hours = High chance of preventing sharing
🚫 DO NOT PAY - All 12 victims who paid were still threatened again for more money
✅ Platforms cooperate with law enforcement - WhatsApp/Tinder provided data within 48 hours
👮 Police take these cases SERIOUSLY - Dedicated women officers available
📱 Digital trail is strong - IP logs + device data = Quick arrests
💪 Speaking up helps others - This arrest saved future victims

features: Real court case studies with evidence details
metadata.source: IT Act 2000 + Amendments, Indian Penal Code 1860, National Cyber Crime Portal Guidelines, Real Court Cases 2023
metadata.legal_disclaimer: This is educational content for cyber crime awareness. For legal advice, consult a qualified lawyer. Crime classification is based on general patterns - each case is unique and requires proper investigation.
